FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE 
December 28, 1988 
#61 
P.l 
CONTACT: Rebecca Gil 
(714) 920-1902 
ASSEMBLYMAN JERRY EAVES 
BRINGS GOVERNMENT TO THE PEOPLE 
You oan learn to out through the bureaucratio red-tape in a snip at 
Assemblyman Jerry Eaves' Second Annual Government on Saturday, Maroh 4, 
1989 from 10:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. at Central city Mallon Seoond street, 
between G and E Streets in San Bernardino. "This event is the equivalent 
of Sacramento ooming to San Bernardino for a day," said Assemblyman 
Jerry Eaves. 
It will be a marketplace brimming with government information. 
"Shoppers" can get updates on just about every benefit program for 
children and seniors and on controversial issues such as the insurance 
initiative proposition 103. A new twist at Assemblyman Jerry Eaves 
Second Annual Government Day is that agencies will be recruiting new 
employees. So you can come to Government Day prepared to complain or to 
court the Government. 
Scores of goverr.ment staffers are giVing up their saturdays "to meet 
with the people ll in a fair-like atmosphere throughout the mall. Enthused 
by the success of the 1st Annual Gov~rnment Day, this year's 
partiCipation is expected to exceed that of the over 70 government 
offices which took part last year. Every level of government, federal, 
state, county and local will be represented. Having been endorsed by the 
County Superintendent of Schools and every local school superintendent, 
the educational value of Government Day is inoreaible. 
Another interesting aspect of Government Day is the interaction which 
will take place between the government staffers. 
"If you want to know 
what happens when the Internal Revenue Servioe talks to the State 
Franchise Tax Board, or when the Department of Motor vehicles talks with 
the Department of Insurance -- come to Government Day and find out. It 
promises to be a great dayl," said Assemblyman Jerry Eaves.

SATUIiOAY. Marcn 26, 1968 The SUM fA 1: 
BROOKS JOHNSON I Publisher 
R08ERT W. RITTER I executive Editor 
, MIKE WHITEHEAD I Managfng Editor 
MARIE SAULSBURY I Managing Editor/Sunday 
RICHARD S. KIMBALL I Editorial Page Sditor 
Have, some more 
'Government Days' 
Assemblyman Jerry Eaves' Government 
, Day concept has proved successful. 
, His idea was to assemble as many gov-
ernment agencies as possible in one place at 
one time, to provide a sort of supermarket 
for information for citizens. 
This he did last weekend at San Bernar-
dino's Central City Mall. Seventy"seven 
agencies, offering information on everything 
from university courses to law enforcement, 
set up booths for the event. 
The event went so well, Eaves, D .. Rialto, 
said, "I'm going to recommend every assem-
blyman in Sacramento do the same thing in 
his or her district at least once a year." 
, 
Eaves colleagues should follow his ad .. 
. vice. Government Day is a helpful way to 
bring agencies closer to the people they 
serve . 
.' 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
February 14, 1989 
Honorable Jerry Eaves 
Assemblymember, Sixty-Sixth District 
state Capitol 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-094 
Dear Assemblymember Eaves: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on February 8, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact John McLean an attorney in the Legal Division, 
directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329.) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:plh 
V~~y truly yours, 
~~,,( P, 
}t1 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
" 
( 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804#0807 • (916)322#5660

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a mass mailing regarding Assemblyman Eaves' Government Day event violated the Political Reform Act's mass mailing prohibition.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised by telephone that the mailing did not violate the mass mailing prohibition under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks traditional formal advice letter structure with verbatim QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. Instead contains a memorandum stating telephone advice was given, confirming compliance with mass mailing prohibition. Actual question inferred from context of mailing materials and Government Day event documentation included in file. No formal written confirmation was prepared as noted in memorandum.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a mass mailing regarding Assemblyman Eaves' Government Day event violated the Political Reform Act's mass mailing prohibition.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advised by telephone that the mailing did not violate the mass mailing prohibition under the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter 89-094 regarding Assemblyman Jerry Eaves' inquiry about whether a mass mailing for his Government Day event violated the Political Reform Act's mass mailing rules. The FPPC provided telephone advice confirming the mailing was compliant.
